values.name: ALLOW
values.description:
Allow: Intents can be made for claims, but claims can also be made without
intents.

values.name: DENY
values.description: Deny: Intents cannot be made for claims for the collection.

values.name: REQUIRED
values.description:
Required: Claims cannot be made without an associated intent. An intent is
mandatory before a claim can be submitted.

enums.name: DisputeStatus
enums.description: DisputeStatus is the lifecycle state of a dispute.

values.name: DISPUTE_STATUS_OPEN
values.description:
Open: dispute has been filed and is awaiting adjudication. Targeted
agent is blocked from submitting / withdrawing on this collection until
resolved.

values.name: DISPUTE_STATUS_AWARDED
values.description:
Awarded: dispute upheld; the targeted party was found in the wrong.
No further disputes against this (subject_id, target_role) are allowed.

values.name: DISPUTE_STATUS_DISMISSED
values.description:
Dismissed: dispute rejected; targeted party was vindicated.
Other parties may file new disputes against the same (subject_id,
target_role) with new evidence.

enums.name: DisputeTargetRole
enums.description:
DisputeTargetRole identifies which party of a claim a dispute is filed
against. A dispute targets exactly one role; to dispute both the submitter
and the evaluator of the same claim, file two separate disputes.

values.name: DISPUTE_TARGET_ROLE_SUBMITTER
values.description: Submitter: the service agent that submitted the claim

values.name: DISPUTE_TARGET_ROLE_EVALUATOR
values.description: Evaluator: the evaluation agent that evaluated the claim

values.name: FLAGGED
values.description:
Flagged: non-terminal "I am declining to make a final call" outcome.
Payment does not fire. The flagger or any other authorized evaluator
can subsequently re-evaluate the claim to a terminal status (APPROVED
/ REJECTED / INVALIDATED) when more information is available. FLAGGED
counts against AgentQuota the same as a terminal evaluation.

values.name: ACTIVE
values.description:
Active: Intent is created and active, payments have been transferred to
escrow if there is any

values.name: FULFILLED
values.description:
Fulfilled: Intent is fulfilled, was used to create a claim and funds will
be released on claim APPROVAL, or funds will be reverted on claim REJECTION
or DISPUTE

values.name: EXPIRED
values.description:
Expired: Intent has expired, payments have been transferred back out of
escrow

values.name: PROMISED
values.description: Promised: Agent is contracted to receive payment

values.name: AUTHORIZED
values.description: Authorized: Authz set up, no guarantee

values.name: GUARANTEED
values.description: Guaranteed: Escrow set up with funds blocked

values.name: PAID
values.description: Paid: Funds have been paid

values.name: FAILED
values.description: Failed: Payment failed, most probably due to insufficient funds

values.name: DISPUTED_PAYMENT
values.description: DisputedPayment: Payment disputed

messages.name: AdjudicationDid
messages.description:
AdjudicationDid represents a single adjudicator entry on a Collection's
dispute whitelist. Each entry pairs a DID with its own reward percentage —
adjudicators self-set their fee, turning adjudication into a competitive
market (lower-fee adjudicators may attract more volume, higher-fee may
trade on reputation). The chain does not enforce who adjudicates a given
dispute; whichever whitelisted DID submits MsgAdjudicateDispute first wins.

fields.name: did
fields.description:
did is the adjudicator's DID. Must appear in this list to be allowed
to settle disputes on the collection.

fields.name: reward_percentage
fields.description:
reward_percentage is the share (LegacyDec, range [0, 100]) of each
actual penalty payout that goes to THIS adjudicator when they settle a
dispute. The remainder goes to the dispute winner.

messages.name: AgentDepositBalance
messages.description:
AgentDepositBalance is an agent's rolling performance-deposit balance for
a single collection. Held inside the collection's existing escrow account
(no separate escrow). Topped up via MsgAddPerformanceDeposit, drained on
adjudicated dispute losses, withdrawable when no active dispute targets
this agent on this collection.

fields.name: collection_id
fields.description: collection_id this balance belongs to.

fields.name: agent_address
fields.description: agent_address is the account this balance is held for.

fields.name: amount
fields.description: amount is the current balance in escrow on this agent's behalf.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: withdrawable_at
fields.description:
withdrawable_at is the earliest block time at which
MsgWithdrawPerformanceDeposit may be issued against this balance. Rolled
forward to max(current, now + collection.min_deposit_period) on each
top-up so an agent cannot deposit + submit + withdraw atomically and
leave nothing at stake before a dispute can land. Zero on balances
created under a collection with min_deposit_period == 0 (no lock).
The slash path is not gated by this lock; only voluntary withdrawal is.

messages.name: CW20Output
messages.description: CW20Output represents a CW20 token output for split payments

fields.name: address
fields.description: address is the address of the recipient

fields.name: contract_address
fields.description: contract_address is the address of the contract

fields.name: amount
fields.description:
amount is the amount of the token to transfer
chose uint64 for now as amounts should be small enough to fit in a
uint64(max 18446744073709551615)

fields.name: amount
fields.description:
chose uint64 for now as amounts should be small enough to fit in a
uint64(max 18446744073709551615)

fields.name: collection_id
fields.description: collection_id indicates to which Collection this claim belongs

fields.name: agent_did
fields.description: agent is the DID of the agent submitting the claim

fields.name: submission_date
fields.description: submissionDate is the date and time that the claim was submitted on-chain

fields.name: claim_id
fields.description: claimID is the unique identifier of the claim in the cid hash format

fields.name: evaluation
fields.description: evaluation is the result of the claim evaluation

fields.name: payments_status
fields.description: payments_status is the status of the payments for the claim

fields.name: use_intent
fields.description: intent_id is the id of the intent for this claim, if any

fields.name: amount
fields.description:
custom amount specified by service agent for claim approval
NOTE: if all amounts are empty then collection default is used
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: cw20_payment
fields.description:
custom cw20 payments specified by service agent for claim approval
NOTE: if all amounts are empty then collection default is used
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: cw1155_payment
fields.description:
custom cw1155 payments specified by service agent for claim approval
NOTE: if all amounts are empty then collection default is used
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: cw1155_intent_payment
fields.description:
If intent was used, this is the cw20_payment equivalent but with amounts
per token_id to transfer the same tokens to and from the escrow account
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: member_address
fields.description:
member_address is the team member this claim is on behalf of, if any.
Copied from intent when use_intent is true. Used for budget restoration
on claim rejection/dispute/invalidation.

fields.name: evaluation_history
fields.description:
evaluation_history holds prior evaluations for this claim in chronological
order. The most recent evaluation always lives in `evaluation`; only
superseded entries are appended here. Empty for claims that have been
evaluated at most once. Populated when an evaluator FLAGS a claim and a
subsequent evaluation (flag-then-flag chain or terminal finalisation)
moves the prior evaluation into history.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: rejection
fields.description: PaymentStatus penalty = 5;

fields.name: id
fields.description: collection id is the incremented internal id for the collection of claims

fields.name: entity
fields.description: entity is the DID of the entity for which the claims are being created

fields.name: admin
fields.description:
admin is the account address that will authorize or revoke agents and
payments (the grantor), and can update the collection

fields.name: protocol
fields.description: protocol is the DID of the claim protocol

fields.name: start_date
fields.description: startDate is the date after which claims may be submitted

fields.name: end_date
fields.description:
endDate is the date after which no more claims may be submitted (no endDate
is allowed)

fields.name: quota
fields.description: quota is the maximum number of claims that may be submitted, 0 is unlimited

fields.name: count
fields.description: count is the number of claims already submitted (internally calculated)

fields.name: evaluated
fields.description:
evaluated is the number of claims that have been evaluated (internally
calculated)

fields.name: approved
fields.description:
approved is the number of claims that have been evaluated and approved
(internally calculated)

fields.name: rejected
fields.description:
rejected is the number of claims that have been evaluated and rejected
(internally calculated)

fields.name: disputed
fields.description:
disputed is the number of claims that have disputed status (internally
calculated)

fields.name: state
fields.description: state is the current state of this Collection (open, paused, closed)

fields.name: payments
fields.description:
payments is the amount paid for claim submission, evaluation, approval, or
rejection

fields.name: signer
fields.description: signer address

fields.name: invalidated
fields.description:
invalidated is the number of claims that have been evaluated as invalid
(internally calculated)

fields.name: escrow_account
fields.description:
escrow_account is the escrow account address for this collection created at
collection creation, current purpose is to transfer payments to escrow
account for GUARANTEED payments through intents

fields.name: intents
fields.description:
intents is the option for intents for this collection (allow, deny,
required)

fields.name: flagged
fields.description:
flagged is the cumulative number of times any claim in this collection
has been flagged by an evaluator. Never decremented — it is an
event-count metric, not a current-state count.

fields.name: flagged_active
fields.description:
flagged_active is the number of claims currently in FLAGGED state
(incremented when a claim becomes FLAGGED, decremented when it
transitions to a terminal evaluation status).

fields.name: service_agent_deposit_required
fields.description:
service_agent_deposit_required is the minimum performance deposit balance
a service agent must hold on this collection to submit claims. Empty /
zero means no deposit gate for service agents.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: evaluator_deposit_required
fields.description:
evaluator_deposit_required is the minimum performance deposit balance an
evaluator must hold on this collection to submit evaluations. Empty /
zero means no deposit gate for evaluators.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: dispute_deposit_amount
fields.description:
dispute_deposit_amount is the stake a disputer attaches to MsgDisputeClaim.
Held with the dispute record; refunded on AWARDED; becomes the penalty
pot on DISMISSED. Empty / zero means no disputer stake required.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: penalty_amount_per_dispute
fields.description:
penalty_amount_per_dispute is the fixed penalty applied on AWARDED. If
empty / zero, the adjudicator sets the penalty per-resolution (bounded by
the loser's deposit-required amount). At collection-validation time, if
set, must be ≤ each non-empty deposit-required field.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: disputes_open
fields.description:
disputes_open is the number of currently-OPEN disputes against any
claim in this collection (internally calculated).

fields.name: disputes_awarded
fields.description:
disputes_awarded is the cumulative number of disputes ever resolved as
AWARDED on this collection (internally calculated, never decremented).

fields.name: disputes_dismissed
fields.description:
disputes_dismissed is the cumulative number of disputes ever resolved as
DISMISSED on this collection (internally calculated, never decremented).

fields.name: min_deposit_period
fields.description:
min_deposit_period is the minimum duration a performance deposit must
remain locked after the most recent top-up before
MsgWithdrawPerformanceDeposit can be issued. Closes the in-same-tx exploit
where an agent could deposit, submit/evaluate, and withdraw atomically —
leaving zero economic stake at dispute time. Set to zero duration to
disable the lock. Each MsgAddPerformanceDeposit rolls
AgentDepositBalance.withdrawable_at forward to max(current, now +
min_deposit_period); the slash path is not gated by this lock.

fields.name: adjudicators
fields.description:
adjudicators is the whitelist of approved adjudicator DIDs paired with
their reward percentages. Each entry carries its own reward_percentage,
letting adjudicators self-set their fees (a competitive market). The
chain does not enforce who adjudicates a given dispute — whichever
whitelisted DID lands MsgAdjudicateDispute first wins; the percentage
applied is that adjudicator's entry. Required to be non-empty if any
deposit-required / dispute_deposit / penalty field is set; clearing
it is blocked while disputes_open > 0.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: type
fields.description: type is expressed as an integer, interpreted by the client

fields.name: target_role
fields.description:
target_role is the party of the claim this dispute is filed against
(submitter or evaluator). Exactly one role per dispute; to dispute both
parties of the same claim, file two disputes. UNSPECIFIED only appears
on legacy disputes migrated from pre-v7 state.

fields.name: disputer_address
fields.description:
disputer_address is the account that filed the dispute and locked the
dispute_deposit. Receives the deposit back on AWARDED, or 80% of the
penalty (loser balance) on AWARDED; loses the deposit (split 80/20
with adjudicator) on DISMISSED.

fields.name: disputer_did
fields.description: disputer_did is the DID of the disputer at filing time.

fields.name: dispute_deposit
fields.description:
dispute_deposit is the amount the disputer locked at filing, equal to
collection.dispute_deposit_amount at the time of filing. Held in the
collection escrow account.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: submitted_at
fields.description: submitted_at is the block time the dispute was filed.

fields.name: status
fields.description: status is the current lifecycle state.

fields.name: resolution
fields.description: resolution is populated on adjudication (AWARDED or DISMISSED).

fields.name: uri
fields.description: dispute link ***.ipfs

messages.name: DisputeResolution
messages.description:
DisputeResolution captures the outcome of MsgAdjudicateDispute. Records
the intended penalty (what the adjudicator/collection prescribed) and the
actual penalty paid (capped at the loser's available deposit balance —
may be less than intended if a prior dispute already drained the balance).

fields.name: adjudicator_did
fields.description:
adjudicator_did is the DID that adjudicated; must be in the collection's
adjudication_entity_dids whitelist.

fields.name: adjudicator_address
fields.description:
adjudicator_address is the signer of MsgAdjudicateDispute. Either an
entity account belonging to adjudicator_did, or a key registered on the
adjudicator_did DID document.

fields.name: adjudicator_payout_address
fields.description:
adjudicator_payout_address is where the 20% reward (or 100% if no
winner share) was actually paid out — either the AdjudicatorRevenue
entity account (auto-created) when the DID is an entity DID with module
accounts, or adjudicator_address otherwise.

fields.name: data
fields.description:
data is the structured payload the adjudicator attached to the
resolution — same shape as the dispute filing's DisputeData. Lets the
adjudicator pin an opinion document (IPFS/matrix uri + proof/cid), declare
its MIME type, and flag encryption — mirroring how disputers attach
evidence at filing time.

fields.name: intended_penalty
fields.description:
intended_penalty is the penalty the adjudicator selected (or the
collection's penalty_amount_per_dispute if fixed). May exceed what was
actually paid if the loser's balance was insufficient.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: actual_penalty_paid
fields.description:
actual_penalty_paid is what was actually slashed from the loser's
balance (or the dispute_deposit, on DISMISSED). Always ≤ intended.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: winner_amount
fields.description:
winner_amount is the portion of actual_penalty_paid that went to the
dispute winner (disputer on AWARDED, target agent on DISMISSED).
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: adjudicator_amount
fields.description:
adjudicator_amount is the portion of actual_penalty_paid that went to
the adjudicator (governed by collection.adjudicator_reward_percentage).
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: winner_address
fields.description: winner_address is the address that received the winner_amount.

fields.name: loser_address
fields.description: loser_address is the address whose balance / deposit was slashed.

fields.name: claim_id
fields.description: claim_id indicates which Claim this evaluation is for

fields.name: collection_id
fields.description:
collection_id indicates to which Collection the claim being evaluated
belongs to

fields.name: oracle
fields.description: oracle is the DID of the Oracle entity that evaluates the claim

messages.name: AgentDepositBalanceCreatedEvent
messages.description:
AgentDepositBalanceCreatedEvent is emitted when an agent's
performance-deposit balance is created for the first time (i.e. the first
MsgAddPerformanceDeposit for a given (collection, agent) pair).

messages.name: AgentDepositBalanceRemovedEvent
messages.description:
AgentDepositBalanceRemovedEvent is emitted when the agent's balance is fully
drained (full withdrawal or full slash brings amount to zero) and the KV
entry is deleted. Carries the final balance state (with zero amount) so the
indexer can archive the entry. Mirrors MemberBudgetRemovedEvent.

messages.name: AgentDepositBalanceUpdatedEvent
messages.description:
AgentDepositBalanceUpdatedEvent is emitted on any state change to an existing
agent performance-deposit balance: subsequent top-up, partial withdrawal, or
slashing on adjudicated dispute loss. Carries the post-state balance. The
indexer can derive the delta by diffing against the previous on-record value;
the reason can be inferred from the enclosing tx's Msg type
(MsgAddPerformanceDeposit / MsgWithdrawPerformanceDeposit /
MsgAdjudicateDispute).

messages.name: MsgAddPerformanceDeposit
messages.description:
MsgAddPerformanceDeposit tops up an agent's performance-deposit balance on
a collection. Funds move from agent_address → collection.escrow_account.
Permitted regardless of whether the agent currently has active disputes —
only withdrawals and new submissions are gated.

fields.name: agent_address
fields.description:
agent_address is the owner of the balance being topped up; also the
payer. Anyone can fund their own balance on any collection (no authz
grant required from the collection admin).

fields.name: new_balance
fields.description: new_balance is the agent's resulting balance after this top-up.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: MsgAdjudicateDispute
messages.description:
MsgAdjudicateDispute settles a dispute. Signed by adjudicator_address,
which must be either an EntityAccount belonging to adjudicator_did, OR a
key registered on the adjudicator_did DID document (capability invocation
/ authentication verification method). adjudicator_did must be in the
collection's adjudication_entity_dids whitelist.

AWARDED: loser is the targeted agent of the dispute. Penalty is debited
from their AgentDepositBalance (capped at available). 80% (by default)
goes to the disputer, 20% to the adjudicator payout address. The
disputer's dispute_deposit is returned to them in full.

DISMISSED: loser is the disputer. The disputer's dispute_deposit is the
pot. 80% goes to the targeted agent (vindicated), 20% to the adjudicator
payout address.

The penalty amount may be left empty by the caller if the collection has
penalty_amount_per_dispute set, in which case the collection value is
used. Otherwise the caller must supply a penalty bounded by the loser's
deposit-required (or dispute_deposit_amount for DISMISSED).

fields.name: subject_id
fields.description: subject_id of the dispute being adjudicated.

fields.name: target_role
fields.description:
target_role of the dispute being adjudicated. Together with subject_id,
identifies the dispute uniquely (one OPEN dispute per pair).

fields.name: adjudicator_did
fields.description: adjudicator_did must be in collection.adjudication_entity_dids.

fields.name: adjudicator_address
fields.description:
adjudicator_address is the signer. Must be authorized by adjudicator_did
(entity account OR DID-registered key).

fields.name: outcome
fields.description: outcome must be AWARDED or DISMISSED; OPEN is rejected.

fields.name: data
fields.description:
data is the structured payload the adjudicator wants recorded on the
resolution — symmetric with MsgDisputeClaim.data. The keeper stores
this verbatim on DisputeResolution.data, so adjudicators can pin a
signed opinion doc (IPFS/matrix uri + proof/cid), declare its MIME type,
and flag encryption.

fields.name: penalty_amount
fields.description:
penalty_amount: if collection has a fixed penalty_amount_per_dispute,
this field is ignored. Otherwise must be set and ≤ loser's role
deposit-required (AWARDED) or ≤ dispute_deposit_amount (DISMISSED).
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: actual_penalty_paid
fields.description:
actual_penalty_paid is what was actually slashed; may be less than the
intended penalty if the loser's balance was insufficient.
fields.label: repeated

fields.description: The service agent's DID (Decentralized Identifier).

messages.name: MsgSetCollectionMembers
messages.description:
MsgSetCollectionMembers adds or updates one or more member budgets on a
collection in a single transaction. For each member entry:
  - If the member already exists and reset_period_spent is false: budget
    limits (period, spend_limit, cw20_spend_limit) are updated, but
    period_spent and period_reset_at are preserved. The current period
    continues with the new limits applied immediately.
  - If the member already exists and reset_period_spent is true:
    period_spent is cleared and period_reset_at is set to now + period.
    This starts a fresh cycle from the current block time.
  - If the member is new: created with period_spent = zero and
    period_reset_at = now + period.

Handler will reject any member entry where all spend limits are zero —
use MsgRemoveCollectionMembers instead. Duplicate member addresses within
a single message are rejected.

messages.name: MsgUpdateCollectionDisputeConfig
messages.description:
MsgUpdateCollectionDisputeConfig updates the dispute / performance-deposit
configuration on a collection. All fields are replacements, not merges —
the caller must send the full desired state. To "clear" a field, send it
empty / zero. The handler enforces:
  - if any deposit-required / penalty / disputer-stake field is non-empty,
    adjudication_entity_dids must be non-empty;
  - penalty_amount_per_dispute (if set) must be ≤ each non-empty
    deposit-required field;
  - adjudicator_reward_percentage in [0, 100].
Changing config does NOT affect in-flight disputes (each dispute
snapshots config it cares about at filing / adjudication time).

messages.name: EpochInfo
messages.description:
EpochInfo is a struct that describes the data going into
a timer defined by the x/epochs module.

fields.name: identifier
fields.description: identifier is a unique reference to this particular timer.

fields.name: start_time
fields.description:
start_time is the time at which the timer first ever ticks.
If start_time is in the future, the epoch will not begin until the start
time.

fields.name: duration
fields.description:
duration is the time in between epoch ticks.
In order for intended behavior to be met, duration should
be greater than the chains expected block time.
Duration must be non-zero.

fields.name: current_epoch
fields.description:
current_epoch is the current epoch number, or in other words,
how many times has the timer 'ticked'.
The first tick (current_epoch=1) is defined as
the first block whose blocktime is greater than the EpochInfo start_time.

fields.name: current_epoch_start_time
fields.description:
current_epoch_start_time describes the start time of the current timer
interval. The interval is (current_epoch_start_time,
current_epoch_start_time + duration] When the timer ticks, this is set to
current_epoch_start_time = last_epoch_start_time + duration only one timer
tick for a given identifier can occur per block.

NOTE! The current_epoch_start_time may diverge significantly from the
wall-clock time the epoch began at. Wall-clock time of epoch start may be
>> current_epoch_start_time. Suppose current_epoch_start_time = 10,
duration = 5. Suppose the chain goes offline at t=14, and comes back online
at t=30, and produces blocks at every successive time. (t=31, 32, etc.)
* The t=30 block will start the epoch for (10, 15]
* The t=31 block will start the epoch for (15, 20]
* The t=32 block will start the epoch for (20, 25]
* The t=33 block will start the epoch for (25, 30]
* The t=34 block will start the epoch for (30, 35]
* The **t=36** block will start the epoch for (35, 40]

fields.name: epoch_counting_started
fields.description:
epoch_counting_started is a boolean, that indicates whether this
epoch timer has began yet.

fields.name: current_epoch_start_height
fields.description:
current_epoch_start_height is the block height at which the current epoch
started. (The block height at which the timer last ticked)

fields.name: epoch_number
fields.description: Epoch number, starting from 1.

fields.name: epoch_number
fields.description: Epoch number, starting from 1.

fields.name: start_time
fields.description: The start timestamp of the epoch.

messages.name: ModuleParams
messages.description:
ModuleParams defines global, module-wide parameters that apply across every
liquid staking pool.

fields.name: min_liquid_stake_amount
fields.description:
min_liquid_stake_amount is the minimum amount of native tokens that can
be liquid-staked into any pool, applied across the entire module to
minimise decimal loss and gas waste from dust amounts.

fields.name: module_paused
fields.description:
module_paused is a global emergency kill switch. When true, ALL pools
are paused regardless of their per-pool paused flag: liquid_stake,
liquid_unstake, autocompounding, rebalancing, and BeginBlocker logic
are halted module-wide. Used for migrations or critical incidents.

messages.name: NetAmountState
messages.description:
NetAmountState holds the raw amounts and exchange rates for a single pool.
Computed on the fly from pool state every time (never persisted), and used
for unstake-rate calculation and queries. Each pool has its own independent
NetAmountState.

fields.name: stake_rate
fields.description:
stake_rate is the mint rate when staking into this pool.
Always 1.0: 1 native token mints 1 LST regardless of accrued rewards.

fields.name: unstake_rate
fields.description:
unstake_rate is the burn rate when unstaking from this pool, equal to
net_amount / stkixo_total_supply. Diverges from 1.0 as rewards accrue
or slashing occurs.

fields.name: stkixo_total_supply
fields.description: stkixo_total_supply is the total supply of this pool's LST denom.

fields.name: net_amount
fields.description:
net_amount is this pool's total liquid tokens + total unbonding balance,
measured at its own proxy account.

fields.name: total_del_shares
fields.description:
total_del_shares is the sum of delegation shares held by this pool's
proxy account across all of its liquid validators.

fields.name: total_liquid_tokens
fields.description:
total_liquid_tokens is the token-equivalent of total_del_shares with
slashing applied, summed across this pool's liquid validators.

fields.name: total_remaining_rewards
fields.description:
total_remaining_rewards is the sum of unwithdrawn staking rewards owed
to this pool's proxy account from all its liquid validators.

fields.name: total_unbonding_balance
fields.description:
total_unbonding_balance is the sum of unbonding amounts (slashing
applied) for this pool's proxy account.

fields.name: proxy_acc_balance
fields.description:
proxy_acc_balance is the spendable native-token balance currently sitting
in this pool's proxy account (rewards withdrawn but not yet redelegated).

messages.name: Params
messages.description:
Params defines the legacy single-pool params layout.

DEPRECATED: kept only so the v7 upgrade migration can unmarshal pre-upgrade
state from the KV store. New code must use ModuleParams (global) and Pool
(per-pool). Do not reference this from new application logic.

messages.name: Pool
messages.description:
Pool defines a single liquid staking instance with its own LST denom,
validator whitelist, admin, and fee configuration. Each pool maintains an
independent NetAmount/Supply ratio, so two pools' LST tokens are NOT
fungible with each other and may diverge in IXO value over time.

fields.name: pool_id
fields.description:
pool_id is the unique, immutable identifier for this pool (e.g. "zero",
"qi"). Used in storage keys, message routing, and proxy account
derivation. Set at pool creation; cannot be changed.

fields.name: liquid_bond_denom
fields.description:
liquid_bond_denom is the denomination of the LST minted by this pool
(e.g. "uzero"). Must be globally unique across all pools. Immutable.

fields.name: proxy_account_address
fields.description:
proxy_account_address is the bech32-encoded address of the per-pool
delegation proxy account. All delegations, redelegations, unbondings,
and reward withdrawals for this pool flow through this account.
Derived deterministically from pool_id at creation; immutable.
For the legacy "zero" pool migrated from pre-v7 state, this field
holds the original LiquidStakeProxyAcc address so existing
delegations are preserved without state migration.

fields.name: whitelisted_validators
fields.description:
whitelisted_validators are the validators eligible for delegation from
this pool's proxy account. Target weights must sum to 10000.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: unstake_fee_rate
fields.description:
unstake_fee_rate is deducted from the unbonding amount when an unstake
is requested against this pool.

fields.name: fee_account_address
fields.description:
fee_account_address is the bech32-encoded address that accumulates the
autocompound fee for this pool.

fields.name: autocompound_fee_rate
fields.description:
autocompound_fee_rate is the fraction of accrued staking rewards taken
by this pool as a protocol fee on each autocompound epoch and sent to
fee_account_address.

fields.name: whitelist_admin_address
fields.description:
whitelist_admin_address is the bech32-encoded address authorised to
update this pool's whitelisted_validators, weighted_rewards_receivers,
paused flag, and other mutable pool fields. Governance can also update
these fields. The admin is also the only address allowed to call
LiquidStake against this pool.

fields.name: paused
fields.description:
paused is a per-pool safety toggle. When true, this specific pool's
stake/unstake, autocompounding, and rebalancing are halted; other
pools are unaffected. The global ModuleParams.module_paused flag
overrides this and pauses every pool regardless of its per-pool value.

fields.name: weighted_rewards_receivers
fields.description:
weighted_rewards_receivers are the addresses that receive a weighted
share of this pool's autocompound rewards. The sum of weights must
not exceed 1; the remainder is restaked to validators.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: WeightedAddress
messages.description:
WeightedAddress represents an address with a weight assigned to it.
The weight is used to determine the proportion of autocompounding
rewards to be paid to the address.

messages.name: WhitelistedValidator
messages.description:
WhitelistedValidator consists of the validator operator address and the
target weight, which is a value for calculating the real weight to be derived
according to the active status.

fields.name: validator_address
fields.description:
validator_address defines the bech32-encoded address of the whitelisted
validator

fields.name: target_weight
fields.description:
target_weight specifies the target weight for liquid staking, unstaking
amount, which is a value for calculating the real weight to be derived
according to the active status

messages.name: AddLiquidValidatorEvent
messages.description:
AddLiquidValidatorEvent is emitted when a newly whitelisted validator is
activated for a pool.

Field tag preserved from v6 (validator=1); pool_id added at the end.

messages.name: AutocompoundStakingRewardsEvent
messages.description:
AutocompoundStakingRewardsEvent is emitted when a pool's autocompound
epoch hook runs successfully.

Field tag numbers preserved from v6. Amount fields upgraded from
"<amount><denom>" strings to typed Coin.

messages.name: LiquidStakeEvent
messages.description:
LiquidStakeEvent is emitted when a liquid stake is performed against a
specific pool.

Field tag numbers preserved from the pre-v7 single-pool layout
(delegator=1, liquid_amount=2, stk_ixo_minted_amount=3) so wire-level
proto compatibility is preserved for any consumer decoding historical
v6 events with v7 codegen — pool_id simply shows as empty for those.
The liquid_amount and stk_ixo_minted_amount fields were upgraded from
the v6 string format ("100000000uixo") to typed Coin in v7 to remove
regex parsing on the indexer side; the tag is unchanged but the wire
encoding for that tag is now a length-delimited Coin sub-message.

messages.name: LiquidUnstakeEvent
messages.description:
LiquidUnstakeEvent is emitted when a liquid unstake is performed against
a specific pool.

Field tag numbers preserved from v6. completion_time upgraded from RFC3339
string to google.protobuf.Timestamp to match
MsgLiquidUnstakeResponse.completion_time. Amount fields upgraded from
"<amount><denom>" strings to typed Coin.

messages.name: ModuleParamsUpdatedEvent
messages.description:
ModuleParamsUpdatedEvent is emitted when the global ModuleParams change
(either via MsgUpdateModuleParams or MsgSetModulePaused).

messages.name: PoolCreatedEvent
messages.description:
PoolCreatedEvent is emitted when a new pool is registered via
MsgCreatePool.

messages.name: PoolUpdatedEvent
messages.description:
PoolUpdatedEvent is emitted when a pool's configuration changes via any
of: MsgUpdatePool, MsgUpdateWhitelistedValidators,
MsgUpdateWeightedRewardsReceivers, MsgSetPoolPaused.

messages.name: RebalancedLiquidStakeEvent
messages.description:
RebalancedLiquidStakeEvent is emitted after a pool's rebalancing pass.

Field tag numbers preserved from v6. Count fields upgraded from
strconv-formatted strings to typed uint32.

messages.name: GenesisState
messages.description: GenesisState defines the liquidstake module's genesis state.

fields.name: module_params
fields.description: module_params holds the global module-wide parameters.

fields.name: pools
fields.description: pools is the list of every registered liquid staking pool.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: pool_liquid_validators
fields.description:
pool_liquid_validators groups each pool's liquid validators by pool_id.
The pool_id of every entry must match a Pool in pools.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: PoolLiquidValidators
messages.description:
PoolLiquidValidators groups a pool's liquid-validator state for genesis
import/export, since validators are scoped per pool in v7+.

fields.name: pool_id
fields.description: pool_id selects the owning pool.

fields.name: liquid_validators
fields.description: liquid_validators is the set of liquid validators known to the pool.
fields.label: repeated

services.name: Query
services.description: Query defines the gRPC query service for the liquidstake module.

methods.name: ModuleParams
methods.description:
ModuleParams returns the global, module-wide parameters (minimum stake
amount and global pause flag).

methods.name: Pool
methods.description: Pool returns the configuration of a single pool by pool_id.

methods.name: Pools
methods.description: Pools returns every registered pool's configuration, paginated.

methods.name: LiquidValidators
methods.description:
LiquidValidators returns liquid validators (with state) for a single
pool.

methods.name: States
methods.description:
States returns the NetAmountState (rates, supplies, balances) for a
single pool.

messages.name: MsgBurn
messages.description: MsgBurn burns the signer's native uixo tokens. Module-level operation.

fields.name: amount
fields.description: amount must be denominated in uixo.

messages.name: MsgCreatePool
messages.description:
MsgCreatePool registers a new liquid staking pool. Governance only.

The proxy account is derived deterministically from pool_id; pool_id and
liquid_bond_denom must both be globally unique. The newly created pool
starts with an empty whitelisted_validators list, empty
weighted_rewards_receivers, zero fee rates, and paused=false. Validators
must be added with MsgUpdateWhitelistedValidators before staking is
possible (the active-weight-quorum check requires sum >= 33.33%).

fields.name: authority
fields.description: authority must be the governance module address.

fields.name: pool_id
fields.description:
pool_id is the immutable identifier for the new pool. Validated as
lowercase alphanumeric plus '-', length 2..16, globally unique.

fields.name: liquid_bond_denom
fields.description:
liquid_bond_denom is the LST denom for the new pool. Must pass
sdk.ValidateDenom and be globally unique across pools.

fields.name: initial_admin_address
fields.description: initial_admin_address becomes the pool's whitelist_admin_address.

fields.name: initial_fee_account_address
fields.description: initial_fee_account_address becomes the pool's fee_account_address.

fields.name: proxy_account_address
fields.description:
proxy_account_address is the derived bech32 address that will hold this
pool's delegations. Returned for client convenience.

messages.name: MsgLiquidStake
messages.description: MsgLiquidStake liquid-stakes native tokens into a specific pool.

fields.name: delegator_address
fields.description: delegator_address must equal the pool's whitelist_admin_address.

fields.name: pool_id
fields.description:
pool_id selects which pool to stake into and therefore which LST denom
is minted in return.

fields.name: amount
fields.description:
amount is the native staking-token coin to liquid-stake. Its denom must
match the chain's bond denom.

messages.name: MsgLiquidUnstake
messages.description: MsgLiquidUnstake burns LST of a specific pool and initiates unbonding.

fields.name: pool_id
fields.description:
pool_id must correspond to amount.denom (the pool's liquid_bond_denom);
both are required for explicitness and validated to match.

fields.name: amount
fields.description:
amount is the LST coin to burn. Its denom must equal
Pool(pool_id).liquid_bond_denom.

messages.name: MsgSetModulePaused
messages.description:
MsgSetModulePaused toggles the global ModuleParams.module_paused kill
switch. When true, every pool is halted regardless of its per-pool flag.
Governance authority only.

fields.name: authority
fields.description: authority must be the governance module address.

fields.name: is_paused
fields.description: is_paused is the target value of ModuleParams.module_paused.

messages.name: MsgSetPoolPaused
messages.description:
MsgSetPoolPaused toggles a single pool's per-pool paused flag.
Governance or the pool's current admin may call this.

fields.name: authority
fields.description: authority must be either governance or the pool's current admin.

fields.name: pool_id
fields.description: pool_id selects the pool whose paused flag is being set.

fields.name: is_paused
fields.description: is_paused is the target value of Pool.paused.

messages.name: MsgUpdateModuleParams
messages.description: MsgUpdateModuleParams updates the global ModuleParams. Governance only.

fields.name: authority
fields.description: authority must be the governance module address.

fields.name: module_params
fields.description: module_params replaces the current ModuleParams in full.

messages.name: MsgUpdatePool
messages.description:
MsgUpdatePool updates a pool's mutable scalar/address fields. Governance
or the pool's current whitelist_admin_address may call this.

Whitelisted validators, weighted rewards receivers, and the paused flag
each have their own dedicated update message and are NOT touched here.
The pool_id, liquid_bond_denom, and proxy_account_address are immutable
after creation and cannot be changed via this message.

fields.name: authority
fields.description: authority must be either governance or the pool's current admin.

fields.name: pool_id
fields.description: pool_id selects the pool to update.

fields.name: unstake_fee_rate
fields.description: unstake_fee_rate replaces the pool's current unstake fee rate.

fields.name: fee_account_address
fields.description: fee_account_address replaces the pool's current fee account.

fields.name: autocompound_fee_rate
fields.description: autocompound_fee_rate replaces the pool's current autocompound fee rate.

fields.name: whitelist_admin_address
fields.description: whitelist_admin_address replaces the pool's current admin address.

messages.name: MsgUpdateWeightedRewardsReceivers
messages.description:
MsgUpdateWeightedRewardsReceivers replaces a pool's weighted rewards
receivers list. Pool admin only (matches pre-v7 admin-only constraint).

fields.name: authority
fields.description: authority must equal the pool's whitelist_admin_address.

fields.name: pool_id
fields.description: pool_id selects the pool whose receivers are being replaced.

fields.name: weighted_rewards_receivers
fields.description:
weighted_rewards_receivers replaces the pool's receivers list. Sum of
weights must not exceed 1.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: MsgUpdateWhitelistedValidators
messages.description:
MsgUpdateWhitelistedValidators replaces a pool's validator whitelist.
Governance or the pool's current admin may call this. Target weights must
sum to 10000.

fields.name: authority
fields.description: authority must be either governance or the pool's current admin.

fields.name: pool_id
fields.description: pool_id selects the pool whose whitelist is being replaced.

fields.name: whitelisted_validators
fields.description: whitelisted_validators replaces the pool's validator set.
fields.label: repeated

services.name: Msg
services.description: Msg defines the liquid staking Msg service.

methods.name: LiquidStake
methods.description:
LiquidStake delegates native tokens through a pool's proxy account to its
whitelisted validators and mints the pool's LST denom to the delegator.

methods.name: LiquidUnstake
methods.description:
LiquidUnstake burns LST denom of a specific pool and initiates unbonding
from that pool's whitelisted validators back to the delegator.

methods.name: CreatePool
methods.description:
CreatePool registers a new liquid staking pool with its own LST denom,
proxy account, admin, and fee configuration. Governance authority only.

methods.name: UpdateModuleParams
methods.description:
UpdateModuleParams updates global, module-wide parameters (the minimum
liquid stake amount and the global module-paused kill switch).
Governance authority only.

methods.name: UpdatePool
methods.description:
UpdatePool updates the mutable scalar/address fields of a specific pool
(fee rates, fee account, admin address). Governance or the pool's
current admin may call this. Whitelisted validators, weighted rewards
receivers, and the paused flag have their own dedicated update messages.

methods.name: UpdateWhitelistedValidators
methods.description:
UpdateWhitelistedValidators updates the validator whitelist of a specific
pool. Governance or the pool's current admin may call this.

methods.name: UpdateWeightedRewardsReceivers
methods.description:
UpdateWeightedRewardsReceivers updates the weighted rewards receivers
list of a specific pool. The pool's admin may call this.

methods.name: SetPoolPaused
methods.description:
SetPoolPaused toggles the per-pool paused flag for a specific pool.
Governance or the pool's current admin may call this. Other pools are
unaffected.

methods.name: SetModulePaused
methods.description:
SetModulePaused toggles the global module-paused flag in ModuleParams.
When paused, ALL pools are halted regardless of their per-pool flag.
Governance authority only.

methods.name: Burn
methods.description: Burn burns native uixo tokens. Module-level operation, not pool-scoped.

messages.name: MintEpochProvisionsMintedEvent
messages.description:
MintEpochProvisionsMintedEvent is triggered after a epoch is triggered
minting module for inflation.

messages.name: DistributionProportions
messages.description:
DistributionProportions defines the distribution proportions of the minted
denom. In other words, defines which stakeholders will receive the minted
denoms and how much.

fields.name: staking
fields.description:
staking defines the proportion of the minted mint_denom that is to be
allocated as staking rewards.

fields.name: impact_rewards
fields.description:
impact_rewards defines the proportion of the minted mint_denom that is
to be allocated to impact rewards addresses.

fields.name: community_pool
fields.description:
community_pool defines the proportion of the minted mint_denom that is
to be allocated to the community pool.

messages.name: Minter
messages.description: Minter represents the minting state.

fields.name: epoch_provisions
fields.description: epoch_provisions represent rewards for the current epoch.

messages.name: Params
messages.description: Params holds parameters for the x/mint module.

fields.name: mint_denom
fields.description: mint_denom is the denom of the coin to mint.

fields.name: genesis_epoch_provisions
fields.description: genesis_epoch_provisions epoch provisions from the first epoch.

fields.name: epoch_identifier
fields.description: epoch_identifier mint epoch identifier e.g. (day, week).

fields.name: reduction_period_in_epochs
fields.description:
reduction_period_in_epochs the number of epochs it takes
to reduce the rewards.

fields.name: reduction_factor
fields.description:
reduction_factor is the reduction multiplier to execute
at the end of each period set by reduction_period_in_epochs.

fields.name: distribution_proportions
fields.description:
distribution_proportions defines the distribution proportions of the minted
denom. In other words, defines which stakeholders will receive the minted
denoms and how much.

fields.name: weighted_impact_rewards_receivers
fields.description:
weighted_impact_rewards_receivers is the address to receive impact
rewards with weights assigned to each address. The final amount that each
address receives is: epoch_provisions *
distribution_proportions.impact_rewards * Address's Weight.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: minting_rewards_distribution_start_epoch
fields.description:
minting_rewards_distribution_start_epoch start epoch to distribute minting
rewards

messages.name: WeightedAddress
messages.description:
WeightedAddress represents an address with a weight assigned to it.
The weight is used to determine the proportion of the total minted
tokens to be minted to the address.

messages.name: GenesisState
messages.description: GenesisState defines the mint module's genesis state.

fields.name: minter
fields.description: minter is an abstraction for holding current rewards information.

fields.name: params
fields.description: params defines all the parameters of the mint module.

fields.name: reduction_started_epoch
fields.description:
reduction_started_epoch is the first epoch in which the reduction of mint
begins.

messages.name: QueryEpochProvisionsRequest
messages.description:
QueryEpochProvisionsRequest is the request type for the
Query/EpochProvisions RPC method.

messages.name: QueryEpochProvisionsResponse
messages.description:
QueryEpochProvisionsResponse is the response type for the
Query/EpochProvisions RPC method.

fields.name: epoch_provisions
fields.description: epoch_provisions is the current minting per epoch provisions value.

messages.name: QueryParamsRequest
messages.description: QueryParamsRequest is the request type for the Query/Params RPC method.

messages.name: QueryParamsResponse
messages.description: QueryParamsResponse is the response type for the Query/Params RPC method.

fields.name: params
fields.description: params defines the parameters of the module.

services.name: Query
services.description: Query provides defines the gRPC querier service.

methods.name: Params
methods.description: Params returns the total set of minting parameters.

methods.name: EpochProvisions
methods.description: EpochProvisions returns the current minting epoch provisions value.

enums.name: NameStatus
enums.description:
NameStatus represents the lifecycle state of a NameRecord. Names are never
hard-deleted; status transitions are used instead so audit history is
preserved.

values.name: NAME_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED
values.description: NAME_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED is the zero value and must not be persisted.

values.name: NAME_STATUS_ACTIVE
values.description: NAME_STATUS_ACTIVE: the normal resolvable state.

values.name: NAME_STATUS_SUSPENDED
values.description:
NAME_STATUS_SUSPENDED: hidden from resolution, can be restored by
registrar.

values.name: NAME_STATUS_REVOKED
values.description:
NAME_STATUS_REVOKED: terminally taken down, cannot be re-used by the same
owner.

values.name: NAME_STATUS_TOMBSTONED
values.description:
NAME_STATUS_TOMBSTONED: registrar-level take-down for policy/legal reasons;
the (namespace, normalized_name) tuple is permanently burned.

messages.name: NameRecord
messages.description:
NameRecord is a registered name bound to a DID. The uniqueness key for a
NameRecord is (namespace, normalized_name).

fields.name: namespace
fields.description: namespace is the Namespace.name this record belongs to.

fields.name: normalized_name
fields.description:
normalized_name is the canonical form used for uniqueness (trimmed,
lowercased ASCII).

fields.name: display_name
fields.description:
display_name is the original name as supplied by the registrant, preserving
case for presentation.

fields.name: owner_did
fields.description: owner_did is the DID that controls this name. Resolution returns this DID.

fields.name: verified
fields.description:
verified is true if this name was attested to by a registrar/oracle (e.g.
proof of an off-chain identity such as Twitter or KYC).

fields.name: valid_until
fields.description:
valid_until is a unix timestamp; 0 means no expiry. Reserved for future
renewal flow.

fields.name: status
fields.description:
status is the lifecycle state of the record. Resolution should treat
anything other than NAME_STATUS_ACTIVE as not-found.

fields.name: verified_by
fields.description:
verified_by is the DID or address of the registrar/oracle that attested
this record, if any.

fields.name: evidence_hash
fields.description:
evidence_hash is an optional content hash of off-chain attestation
evidence (e.g. a signed JWT or VC).

fields.name: source
fields.description:
source is a free-form tag describing where the verification came from
(e.g. "workos", "ussd", "import", "manual").

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: created_at is the unix timestamp the record was first written.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: updated_at is the unix timestamp of the last write.

messages.name: Namespace
messages.description:
Namespace defines a governed bucket of names (e.g. "yoid", "twitter").
Created and updated only via governance.

fields.name: name
fields.description:
name is the namespace identifier and the uniqueness scope for names
registered under it. Lowercase ASCII recommended.

fields.name: description
fields.description: description is a human-readable summary of the namespace.

fields.name: registrar_accounts
fields.description:
registrar_accounts is the list of bech32 account addresses authorised to
register or moderate names in this namespace on behalf of users.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: allow_self_register
fields.description:
allow_self_register: when true, users can register their own names by
signing MsgRegisterName. When false, only registrars can register.

fields.name: allow_registrar_override
fields.description:
allow_registrar_override: when true, registrars can update or transfer
names regardless of the current owner (used for moderation or assisted
flows). When false, only the owning DID can transfer/update.

fields.name: min_length
fields.description: min_length is the minimum allowed length of the normalized name.

fields.name: max_length
fields.description:
max_length is the maximum allowed length of the normalized name. Must be
greater than zero.

fields.name: regex
fields.description:
regex is an additional ASCII-only character pattern that the normalized
name must match. Empty string means no extra check on top of the default.

fields.name: allow_expiry
fields.description:
allow_expiry: when true, NameRecords in this namespace may have a non-zero
valid_until. Reserved for a future renewal flow.

fields.name: registered_by
fields.description:
registered_by is the address that submitted the tx (owner DID controller
for self-register, registrar account otherwise).

fields.name: authority
fields.description:
authority is the gov module address that created the namespace; lets
indexers attribute namespace changes to a governance proposal.

messages.name: GenesisState
messages.description: GenesisState defines the names module's genesis state.

fields.name: name
fields.description: name is normalized server-side; callers may pass the display form.

services.name: Query
services.description: Query defines the names module gRPC query service.

methods.name: Namespace
methods.description: Namespace returns a single namespace by its name.

methods.name: Namespaces
methods.description: Namespaces returns all namespaces.

methods.name: ResolveName
methods.description:
ResolveName returns the active NameRecord for (namespace, name). The name
is normalized server-side before lookup. Returns not-found if the record
is missing or its status is not ACTIVE.

methods.name: GetName
methods.description:
GetName returns a NameRecord regardless of status. Useful for moderation
and audit tools.

methods.name: NamesByNamespace
methods.description: NamesByNamespace lists records under a namespace (paginated).

methods.name: NamesByOwner
methods.description: NamesByOwner lists every name owned by a DID across all namespaces.

fields.name: authority
fields.description:
authority is the address allowed to create namespaces (the gov module
address).

fields.name: namespace
fields.description: namespace is the full configuration of the new namespace.

fields.name: signer
fields.description:
signer is the bech32 address signing the tx; must control owner_did via
an authentication verification method on the IID document.

fields.name: namespace
fields.description: namespace is the namespace under which the name is being registered.

fields.name: name
fields.description:
name is the display name (preserves case); will be normalized for
uniqueness.

fields.name: owner_did
fields.description: owner_did is the DID this name will resolve to.

fields.name: registrar
fields.description:
registrar is the bech32 address signing the tx; must be in the
namespace's registrar_accounts.

fields.name: verified
fields.description:
verified marks the record as attested. Registrars typically set this true
after off-chain verification; user-driven self-register sets it false.

fields.name: evidence_hash
fields.description: evidence_hash is an optional hash of the off-chain attestation evidence.

fields.name: source
fields.description: source is a free-form tag describing the verification source.

fields.name: normalized_name
fields.description: normalized_name is the canonical form stored.

fields.name: signer
fields.description: signer is either a namespace registrar or the governance authority.

fields.name: reason
fields.description: reason is a free-form string surfaced in the audit event.

fields.name: signer
fields.description:
signer is the bech32 address submitting the transfer; must control the
current owner_did, OR be a registrar when allow_registrar_override is
enabled.

fields.name: new_owner_did

methods.name: CreateNamespace
methods.description: CreateNamespace creates a new namespace. Authority-only (gov).

methods.name: UpdateNamespace
methods.description:
UpdateNamespace replaces an existing namespace's configuration.
Authority-only (gov). All Namespace fields supplied in the message become
the new state.

methods.name: RegisterName
methods.description:
RegisterName lets a user register a name in a namespace that allows
self-registration. The signer must control owner_did.

methods.name: RegisterNameByRegistrar
methods.description:
RegisterNameByRegistrar lets a registrar register a name on behalf of
owner_did. The signer must be a registrar of the namespace.

methods.name: UpdateNameByRegistrar
methods.description:
UpdateNameByRegistrar lets a registrar update the verification metadata
(verified flag, verified_by, evidence_hash, source) of an existing record.
The signer must be a registrar of the namespace. Owner DID is not
changed by this message; use TransferName for that.

methods.name: TransferName
methods.description:
TransferName transfers ownership of a name to another DID. Permitted for
the current owner; permitted for a registrar when the namespace has
allow_registrar_override = true.

methods.name: SetNameStatus
methods.description:
SetNameStatus changes the lifecycle status of a name (suspend, revoke,
tombstone, or restore to active). Permitted for registrars and the
governance authority.

messages.name: AuthnPubKey

fields.name: key_id
fields.description:
The key_id (or credential ID) is a unique identifier for a passkey.
This ID is provided by the authenticator when the passkey is created.
As it is not possible to retrieve the public key from the authenticator
after the passkey is created, if the user loses the public key - id
association, the key_id can be used to retrieve the public key.

fields.name: cose_algorithm
fields.description:
Store the COSE algorithm identifier. Since authn allows multiple different
public key credential algorithm, eg.
-7(ES256) - ECDSA with SHA-256 on the P-256 curve
-257(RS256) - RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 with SHA-256
we need to store the algorithm identifier to be able to verify the
signature according to the algorithm the public key is using.

The country-code must be 2 alphabetic characters, the sub-national-code
can be 1-3 alphanumeric characters, and the postal-code can be up to 64

sub-national-code and postal-code are optional and can be added for
